_about: Phase 2 expansion corpus. Additive to TIG (Phase 1) and humans+math (Phase 1.5). Same discipline: real verified content, public-domain quotes attributed, generic acknowledgments in everyday language. The new dimension is Q&A pairs as natural sentences ('When asked X, answer Y is appropriate') so the lattice sees input-output structure.
math_linear_algebra: A vector is an ordered list of numbers representing magnitude and direction.
A matrix is a rectangular array of numbers arranged in rows and columns.
Matrix multiplication is not commutative: A times B does not generally equal B times A.
The determinant of a 2x2 matrix is a*d minus b*c.
A matrix is invertible if and only if its determinant is nonzero.
The rank of a matrix is the dimension of its column space.
Eigenvalues are scalars lambda such that A times v equals lambda times v for some nonzero vector v.
Eigenvectors point in directions that the matrix only stretches, not rotates.
The trace of a square matrix is the sum of its diagonal entries.
The trace equals the sum of the eigenvalues.
The determinant equals the product of the eigenvalues.
An orthogonal matrix has columns that are mutually perpendicular unit vectors.
A symmetric matrix has real eigenvalues and orthogonal eigenvectors.
Singular value decomposition factors any matrix into U times Sigma times V transpose.
math_group_theory: A group is a set with a binary operation, an identity element, and inverses for every element, where the operation is associative.
An abelian group has a commutative operation.
The integers under addition form an abelian group.
The order of a group is the number of its elements.
Lagrange's theorem says the order of any subgroup divides the order of the group.
Cyclic groups are generated by a single element.
The dihedral group D_n is the group of symmetries of a regular n-gon, with 2n elements.
The symmetric group S_n is the group of permutations of n objects, with n factorial elements.
An isomorphism is a structure-preserving bijection between two groups.
Two groups are isomorphic if and only if they have the same multiplication table up to relabeling.
The kernel of a homomorphism is the preimage of the identity.
Normal subgroups are kernels of homomorphisms.
The quotient group G/N exists when N is a normal subgroup of G.
math_complex_analysis: A complex number is z equals a plus b i, where i squared is negative one.
The modulus of z is the square root of a squared plus b squared.
The argument of z is the angle theta such that z equals r times e to the i theta.
Euler's identity is e to the i pi plus one equals zero.
A complex function is analytic if it is differentiable in a neighborhood.
The Cauchy-Riemann equations characterize analytic functions in terms of partial derivatives.
Cauchy's integral theorem says the contour integral of an analytic function over a closed curve is zero.
The residue theorem evaluates contour integrals via residues at poles.
An entire function is analytic on the whole complex plane.
Liouville's theorem says a bounded entire function is constant.
human_aurelius_more: Marcus Aurelius (Meditations IV.3, public domain): 'You will find rest from vain fancies if you perform every act in life as though it were your last.'
Aurelius (II.5): 'Begin the morning by saying to thyself, I shall meet with the busybody, the ungrateful, arrogant, deceitful... I cannot be injured by any of them.'
Aurelius (VII.59): 'Look within. Within is the fountain of good, and it will ever bubble up if thou wilt ever dig.'
Aurelius (V.16): 'The soul becomes dyed with the color of its thoughts.'
Aurelius (VIII.47): 'If you are distressed by anything external, the pain is not due to the thing itself but to your estimate of it; and this you have the power to revoke at any moment.'
Aurelius (VI.21): 'If any man is able to convince me and show me that I do not think or act right, I will gladly change. For I seek the truth.'
Aurelius (IV.7): 'Reject your sense of injury and the injury itself disappears.'
human_dickinson: Emily Dickinson (public domain): 'Hope is the thing with feathers that perches in the soul.'
Dickinson: 'If I can stop one heart from breaking, I shall not live in vain.'
Dickinson: 'The truth must dazzle gradually, or every man be blind.'
Dickinson: 'I'm nobody. Who are you? Are you nobody too?'
Dickinson: 'Tell all the truth but tell it slant. Success in circuit lies.'
human_whitman_thoreau: Walt Whitman (Song of Myself, 1855, public domain): 'I celebrate myself, and sing myself, and what I assume you shall assume.'
Whitman: 'Do I contradict myself? Very well then I contradict myself, I am large, I contain multitudes.'
Thoreau (Walden, 1854): 'Time is but the stream I go a-fishing in.'
Thoreau: 'The price of anything is the amount of life you exchange for it.'
Thoreau: 'If a man does not keep pace with his companions, perhaps it is because he hears a different drummer.'
human_practical_relationships: An apology has three parts: acknowledging the harm, naming what you did, saying what you will do differently.
Saying 'I was wrong' takes courage and earns trust.
Active listening is repeating back what you heard before responding.
When someone shares a feeling, validate before suggesting solutions.
It is okay to say 'I do not know what to say' and just stay present.
Boundaries are how you protect what matters to you. They are not punishments.
Saying no clearly is kinder than saying yes resentfully.
Friendship is built over time through small consistent acts.
Asking 'how are you, really' invites a deeper conversation than 'how are you'.
When in conflict, focus on the behavior, not the character.
I-statements ('I feel hurt when...') invite dialogue better than you-statements ('You always...').
Repair after rupture is the most important skill in any close relationship.
Some conversations are worth having even when they are uncomfortable.
Forgiveness can release you from carrying weight that is not yours.
Some people earn distance, not your second chance.
human_parenting_aging: Children need both safety and freedom to develop well.
Reading aloud to a child builds language and connection at the same time.
Boredom is the soil where creativity grows.
Saying 'I love you' to children, every day, is not too often.
Acknowledging a child's feeling does not mean indulging the behavior.
Aging well is partly luck and partly habits practiced for decades.
Sleep, movement, social connection, and purpose are the four pillars of healthy aging.
Caring for an aging parent reshapes the family in ways no one prepares for.
Grief from anticipated loss is real grief.
It is possible to honor someone's life without idealizing them.
Legacy is what you taught people, not what you accumulated.
human_work_meaning: Vocation is the place where your gifts and the world's needs meet.
Burnout is not a personal failing. It is a system signal.
Rest is not the reward for finishing. It is part of the work.
Saying no to good things lets you say yes to important things.
Meaningful work does not have to be your job.
Helping one person well is enough for one day.
The opposite of burnout is not vacation. It is sustainable rhythm.
Comparison is the thief of joy.
Your worth is not your productivity.
It is okay to change careers, even multiple times.
Mastery takes years of small consistent practice.
general_geography: Mount Everest is approximately 8849 meters tall.
The Pacific Ocean is the largest ocean, covering about 165 million square kilometers.
The Amazon rainforest spans about 5.5 million square kilometers across nine countries.
The Sahara Desert is the largest hot desert, about 9 million square kilometers.
Antarctica is the southernmost continent and the coldest place on Earth.
The Nile River is approximately 6650 kilometers long.
Lake Baikal in Russia is the deepest lake on Earth at about 1642 meters.
Tokyo is the most populous metropolitan area in the world.
Vatican City is the smallest sovereign state at about 0.49 square kilometers.
The seven continents are Africa, Antarctica, Asia, Australia, Europe, North America, and South America.
The four oceans are Pacific, Atlantic, Indian, and Arctic; some include the Southern Ocean as a fifth.
The Great Barrier Reef off Australia is the largest coral reef system.
general_history_dated: The pyramids at Giza were built around 2500 BCE.
Confucius lived from approximately 551 to 479 BCE.
The Roman Empire fell in 476 CE.
The Magna Carta was sealed in 1215.
The fall of Constantinople was in 1453.
The Reformation began in 1517 with Martin Luther's 95 Theses.
The Declaration of Independence was signed in 1776.
The American Civil War lasted from 1861 to 1865.
World War I lasted from 1914 to 1918.
World War II lasted from 1939 to 1945.
The civil rights march on Washington was August 28, 1963.
The Apollo 11 moon landing was July 20, 1969.
The Berlin Wall fell on November 9, 1989.
general_health_wellness: Adults generally need seven to nine hours of sleep per night.
Drinking water through the day supports concentration and energy.
Walking thirty minutes a day has substantial cardiovascular benefits.
Strength training preserves bone density and muscle mass with age.
Mindfulness practice reduces measurable markers of stress over time.
A balanced diet generally includes vegetables, fruits, whole grains, lean proteins, and healthy fats.
Excessive added sugar correlates with higher risk of metabolic disease.
Regular exercise improves mood through endorphins and neurotransmitter regulation.
Sunlight exposure helps regulate circadian rhythm and vitamin D synthesis.
Social connection is as predictive of long life as not smoking, per longitudinal studies.
Therapy is helpful for many people; seeking help is not weakness.
If you are in crisis, call your local crisis line or 988 in the United States.
Mental health is health. There is no separation.
general_curiosity_kids: The sky appears blue because shorter wavelengths of sunlight scatter more in the atmosphere.
Rainbows form when sunlight refracts through water droplets, separating into colors.
Stars appear to twinkle because turbulence in Earth's atmosphere bends their light.
Lightning happens when electrical charges build up between clouds and the ground and discharge.
Tides are caused by the gravitational pull of the moon and the sun.
Plants make food from sunlight, water, and air through photosynthesis.
Bees pollinate flowers as they collect nectar, helping plants reproduce.
Ants can lift many times their own body weight because of their small body mass.
Octopuses have three hearts and blue blood.
Sharks have been on Earth longer than trees.
The human body has about 60,000 miles of blood vessels.
Light from the Sun takes about eight minutes to reach Earth.
Sound travels faster in water than in air.
Most of the universe is invisible to us, made of dark matter and dark energy.
general_philosophy: Socrates: the unexamined life is not worth living.
Plato: the only thing I know is that I know nothing.
Aristotle: we are what we repeatedly do; excellence is a habit.
Descartes: I think, therefore I am.
Kant: act only according to that maxim by which you can will it to become a universal law.
Hume: a wise man proportions his belief to the evidence.
Nietzsche: he who has a why to live can bear almost any how.
Wittgenstein: the limits of my language mean the limits of my world.
Simone Weil: attention is the rarest and purest form of generosity.
Hannah Arendt: the sad truth is that most evil is done by people who never make up their minds to be good or evil.
Camus: there is but one truly serious philosophical problem, and that is suicide; meaning, judging whether life is worth living.
Confucius: it does not matter how slowly you go as long as you do not stop.
general_listening_followups: How long has this been going on?
Have you talked to anyone else about this?
What does your gut tell you?
What would help you most right now?
Is there a small step you could take today?
What did that mean to you?
Do you want to keep going, or take a breath?
Sometimes there is no answer, only company.
I am not going to say I understand, but I want to hear you.
Take all the time you need.
